passages.title: コンビニのお知らせ
passages.en: From tomorrow, this shop opens at 7 in the morning. And we will also sell new bread. Coffee is only 100 yen. Please come by all means.

passages.title: シェアハウスのメール
passages.en: This is to let you know about tomorrow's cleaning. At 10 in the morning we will all clean together. Please throw away the trash. And then, please make the kitchen clean too, okay?

passages.title: 東京日記
passages.en: Today I went shopping in Shibuya. At a department store I bought a new bag. And then I also drank coffee. The bag was expensive, but I really like it.

passages.title: 区役所からの手紙
passages.en: Your residence card is ready. Next week, please come to the ward office to pick it up. The reception desk is open from 9 to 5. And also, please don't forget your passport, okay?

passages.title: 電車のお知らせ
passages.en: Today the trains are delayed. IC card charging can be done in front of the ticket gate. And also, you can buy paper tickets too. People in a hurry, please don't run, okay?

passages.title: 居酒屋のメニュー
passages.en: Our shop's beer is 350 yen. The recommendation is yakitori. Because it's very delicious, please try it by all means. And also, we serve warm tea for free.

passages.title: クリニックの問診票
passages.en: First-time visitors, please write your name. What is wrong today? Do you have a fever? Also, do you have an allergy to any medicine?

passages.title: アパートの内見メッセージ
passages.en: Hi Tanaka. I want to go and see the apartment tomorrow. This room is good. The rent is 80,000 yen. But is it OK to keep a pet?

passages.title: アルバイトのシフト
passages.en: I will send next week's shift. On Monday and Wednesday, please work at night. Friday is a day off. But because Saturday is busy, please come early.

passages.title: ごみの出し方
passages.en: We are letting you know how to put out the trash. Monday and Thursday are for burnable trash. Please put out plastic bottles on Tuesday. And then, cans and bottles are on Friday, you know.

passages.title: 近所の日記
passages.en: Today I took a walk through the neighborhood park. The weather was nice. Many flowers were blooming. And then, I ran together with a dog. It was really fun.

passages.title: コンビニのアルバイト募集
passages.en: We are recruiting part-timers. Someone who can speak a little Japanese is good. The hourly wage is 1200 yen. And also, people who can work at night are very welcome. Please contact us by phone.

passages.title: シェアハウスのルール
passages.en: Let me explain the share house rules. After 11 at night, please be quiet. You may invite friends. But please don't have parties. Also, please use the kitchen cleanly, okay?

passages.title: シェアハウスの相談メール
passages.en: I'm emailing about the kitchen. Yesterday the dishes were left as they were. When you use them, I'd like you to wash them right away. Also, at night the noise is loud, so please be quiet.

passages.title: 店長からの仕事の説明
passages.en: Tomorrow is your first day of work, isn't it. First, change into your uniform beforehand. When you press this button, the receipt comes out. If there's something you don't understand, ask right away. However, when it's busy, you have to think for yourself.

passages.title: 区役所からのお知らせ
passages.en: This is a notice from the ward office. Within two weeks of moving in, please register. The necessary documents are your residence card and so on. The counter gets crowded, so if we have you come in the morning, it finishes quickly. Also, please check the details on the website.

passages.title: 賃貸契約の注意
passages.en: In this room, you may not keep pets. Also, please do not play instruments after 10 at night. In addition, trash has to be put out on designated days. If you make a hole in the wall, we will have you pay the repair cost. If you are going to move out, please contact us one month in advance.

passages.title: 友だちからのLINE
passages.en: Are you free this Saturday? If it's sunny, want to have a picnic at the nearby park? I'll bring the lunch, okay. But if it's rain, it's off. Let me know by Friday.

passages.title: クリニックからの案内
passages.en: Good work today. If your fever doesn't go down, please come again. When you take this medicine, you might get a little sleepy. Also, please don't drink alcohol. After you finish taking all the medicine, we'll examine you once more. Take care.

passages.title: 区民だより
passages.en: Next month, a summer festival will be held at the ward residents' center. So that the children can enjoy it, we have prepared many games. If you would like to take part, please apply by next Friday. Note that in case of rain, it will be cancelled.

passages.title: ボランティア募集
passages.en: We are recruiting volunteers to clean the river. We're looking for people who will take part. On the day, please come in clothes that are easy to move in. Drinks have been prepared here for you. The collected trash is to be picked up by the city. Also, you may take part together with your children.

passages.title: お詫びのメール
passages.en: We are very sorry for the trouble we caused this time. We will send the product you ordered tomorrow. It should arrive by tomorrow. Also, our shop will cover the shipping fee. Please wait a little longer. From now on, we will be careful.

passages.title: 電車の遅延案内
passages.en: We announce this to our customers. Due to a personal-injury accident, the Yamanote Line is currently delayed. Because of the accident, the trains have stopped moving. For that reason, we are causing you great trouble. We are carrying out restoration work, so please wait for the next train.

passages.title: 資源ごみ回収のお知らせ
passages.en: This is a notice to all residents of Shinjuku Ward. From next month, the collection days for recyclable garbage will change. Please put out PET bottles on Tuesdays, just as before. Also, the collection day for used paper will move to Wednesday. Regarding the sorting method, if anything is unclear, please inquire at the ward office counter. Those who use this new system are asked to confirm the details in advance.

passages.title: 上司からの連絡メール
passages.en: Thank you for your work. This is Tanaka from Sales. I'm writing about next week's meeting. First, please tell Yamada-san to prepare the materials. After that, I will chair the meeting myself. I'm a little nervous about the day, but could everyone please help me out? Thank you for your cooperation.

passages.title: 東京生活の日記
passages.en: Three months have passed since I came to Tokyo. At first my Japanese was poor, and at home I did nothing but play games. However, invited by a friend from my share house, I started attending a Japanese class. Thanks to the people I met there, conversation has become fun for me now. The more I try to speak, the more I improve — that's just how it works. After all, taking action is what matters.

passages.title: シェアハウス会議の記録
passages.en: These are the minutes of the third house meeting. This time, we discussed how to use the shared spaces. First, it was decided that the kitchen must always be cleaned after use. Next, taking out the trash will be done on a rotation. However, if the person on duty is busy, someone else will take it out in their place. Also, not only the living room but the hallway too — let's keep them quiet. The above will take effect from next month.

passages.title: 応募の自己紹介
passages.en: Nice to meet you. My name is Tanaka. I studied design at university, and after graduating I worked for three years as a designer at an advertising company. Making use of that experience, I hope to grow further at your company. Although it is my first time working in Japan, I have kept studying Japanese. For that reason, I believe I can speak with customers without trouble. Please, could you kindly grant me the chance of an interview? Thank you very much.

passages.title: 下北沢の夏祭り
passages.en: According to the Shimokitazawa shopping street's announcement, the customary summer festival will be held this weekend. At the festival, food stalls built by local students will line the street. Meanwhile, at the plaza in front of the station, a Bon dance event is also planned. For the people of the community, this festival is one of the most looked-forward-to events of the year. Note that, in case of rain, it will be postponed to the following day.

passages.title: 設備点検のお知らせ
passages.en: To all tenants. We are writing to inform you about an inspection of the building's facilities. Next Wednesday, from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m., we will inspect the water heater in each room. During the work, hot water will be temporarily unavailable. Accordingly, we ask that you move your valuables to a safe place. Also, if you are not at home on the day, the management company will enter using a spare key. However, we will not enter rooms from which we have received no prior notice. Thank you for your cooperation.

passages.title: お詫びのメール
passages.en: I am truly sorry for the trouble I caused the other day. The delay in delivery was due to my own insufficient checking. It is by no means that I took the matter lightly. Also, I deeply regret having pushed ahead with changes without even consulting you. For that reason, could you kindly give me some time to meet again? From now on, so as not to repeat the same mistake, I will be thorough in my checking work. Thank you very much.

passages.title: キャッシュレスについての意見
passages.en: Recently, cashless payment is spreading more and more in Tokyo. Certainly, it is convenient because you don't have to carry cash around. However, I do not think that every shop should abolish cash right away. Just because it is convenient does not mean it is acceptable to ignore people who can only use cash. This is because, among the elderly, there are many who do not own a smartphone. We cannot simply ignore the inconvenience of such people. In the face of technological progress, society ought to be more cautious.

passages.title: 京都旅行の相談
passages.en: About next month's Kyoto trip — I tried putting together a plan. First, let's decide to take an early-morning bullet train. I want to go around the famous temples before they get crowded, you see. After that, while we're going to Kyoto, shall we also make a side trip to Nara? However, the second day looks like rain, so on that day let's make it the art museum instead. I'll book the hotel early. I'm looking forward to it.
